mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression and recession fears, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within one trading week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below -0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, producing negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to generate negative returns in late-cycle regimes, with forward returns averaging positive in greater than 55 percent of declining INDPRO observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer price inflation reduces expected future rate hikes, lowering equity discount rates and triggering immediate positive S&P 500 price adjustments within one trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of declining inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.4 million units, construction activity contraction signals weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45 percent in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50 percent of joint tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, an accelerating industrial production index confirms supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of recovery regime observations, or the Rank IC falls below -0.03 in out-of-sample testing.
